entry.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
entry.content:
A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
entry.content:
The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Emerging Chiplet Designs Spark Fresh Cybersecurity Challenges
entry.content:
As scaled-down circuits with limited functions redefine computing for AI systems and autonomous vehicles, their flexibility demands new approaches to safeguard critical infrastructure.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Latin America's Cyber Maturity Lags Threat Landscape
entry.content:
The slower pace of upgrades has the unintended impact of creating a haven for attackers, especially for initial access brokers and ransomware gangs.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
entry.content:
After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
entry.content:
Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
entry.content:
Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
entry.content:
A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Enigma Cipher Device Still Holds Secrets for Cyber Pros
entry.content:
The Nazi relic's history is riddled with resilience errors, and those lessons still apply to defending against modern cyber threats.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
entry.content:
The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Spitting Cash: ATM Jackpotting Attacks Surged in 2025
entry.content:
The attacks cost banks more than $20 million in losses last year, as criminals used many of the same tools and tactics they have wielded for more than a decade.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
entry.content:
Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
entry.content:
The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
entry.content:
Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
While this campaign targets crypto wallets and steals money, the methodology has far wider potential that could be used by other attackers. The post Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US Healthcare Diagnostic Firm Says 140,000 Affected by Data Breach
entry.content:
The Everest ransomware group has taken credit for a hacker attack on Vikor Scientific, now called Vanta Diagnostics. The post US Healthcare Diagnostic Firm Says 140,000 Affected by Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Taiwan Security Firm Confirms Flaw Flagged by CISA Likely Exploited by Chinese APTs
entry.content:
The vulnerability in TeamT5 ThreatSonar Anti-Ransomware was recently added to CISA’s KEV catalog. The post Taiwan Security Firm Confirms Flaw Flagged by CISA Likely Exploited by Chinese APTs appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: GitHub Issues Abused in Copilot Attack Leading to Repository Takeover
entry.content:
Attackers can inject malicious instructions in a GitHub Issue that are automatically processed by Copilot when launching a Codespace from that issue. The post GitHub Issues Abused in Copilot Attack Leading to Repository Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: New ‘Sandworm_Mode’ Supply Chain Attack Hits NPM
entry.content:
The malicious code propagates like a worm, poisons AI assistants, exfiltrates secrets, and contains a destructive dead switch. The post New ‘Sandworm_Mode’ Supply Chain Attack Hits NPM appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Could Allow Remote Code Execution
entry.content:
Broadcom has patched several vulnerabilities in VMware Aria Operations, including high-severity flaws. The post VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Could Allow Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Arkanix Stealer’ Malware Disappears Shortly After Debut
entry.content:
Written in C++ and Python, the malware exfiltrates system information, browser data, and steals files. The post ‘Arkanix Stealer’ Malware Disappears Shortly After Debut appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Phishing operation with links to Russia, Armenia compromised Western cargo companies, researchers find
entry.content:
Over a five-month period, the group, dubbed Diesel Vortex, stole more than 1,600 login credentials from accounts at logistics platforms, which allowed thieves to intercept and divert freight shipments and commit check fraud.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US sanctions Russian exploit broker for buying cyber tools stolen from defense contractor
entry.content:
The Treasury Department sanctioned a Russian national and his company for allegedly acquiring eight proprietary cyber tools that were stolen from the defense contractor L3 Harris and sold to "unauthorized" customers.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
entry.content:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Grupo VerdeAzul
entry.content:
Verification alert Listings attributed to VECT have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: VECT | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

entry.title: This is how you do it: Dentist speaks out after practice hit by cyber attack
entry.content:
This could be the smallest breach DataBreaches has reported recently, and yet we are covering it instead of other, much bigger breaches that will undoubtedly generate lots of headlines. Why? Because it represents a refreshing example of quick response and transparency. Dr. Joe McEnhill, owner of Grange Dental Care, said the breach occurred on Thursday... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Ukrainian hackers uncover how Russian drone operators are using Belarus
entry.content:
As seen on InformNapalm: On February 18, 2026, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy imposed sanctions against Belarusian dictator Alyaksandr Lukashenka for his role in escalating and prolonging Russia’s aggressive war against Ukraine. One of the stated reasons is that in the second half of 2025, Russia deployed a system of repeaters for the control of attack... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: The hospitality sector continues to be lucrative targets
entry.content:
The hospitality sector has long been a target of hackers, and it’s a global problem. Here are three recent attacks in the news this week: In the U.S.: Choice Hotels International disclosed a breach affecting franchisees and applicants. Its notification letter states that a “skilled person used social engineering” to gain access on January 14,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Top NATO allies believe cyberattacks on hospitals are an act of war. They’re still struggling to fight back.
entry.content:
Maggie Miller, Dana Nickel and Antoaneta Roussi report: NATO countries’ restrained response to hybrid attacks is at odds with public opinion, new polling shows: Broad swaths of the public in key allied countries say actions such as cyberattacks on hospitals should be considered acts of war. The POLITICO Poll, conducted in the United States, Canada,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Some patients listed as “Charlie Kirk” or dead after major NZ health app MediMap hacked
entry.content:
1News reports: A digital medical records data company has been taken offline after some patient records were modified. Some users’ information had been changed, including to say they were deceased. MediMap is used by some health providers in aged care, disability, hospice and the community to accurately record medication doses. Read more at 1News. MediMap... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hackers threaten to leak 8 million people’s stolen data if Dutch telecom Odido won’t pay ransom
entry.content:
Daniel Verlaan reports: The cybercriminal group Shinyhunters is responsible for hacking Odido. On the dark web, Odido is being pressured to pay the ransom—over a million euros. “This is your final warning,” the hackers write. “Otherwise, we will leak the data.” Shinyhunters confirmed to RTL Nieuws that it was behind the hack and has shown... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Korean cops charge teens over bike hire breach that exposed data on 4.62M riders
entry.content:
Connor Jones reports: Two South Korean teenagers were this week charged with breaching Seoul’s public bike service, Ttareungyi. Identified only as Persons A and B, the pair, now of high school age, allegedly carried out the attack on Ttareungyi in June 2024 and stole data belonging to most of the service’s registered users. Officials said... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Lazarus hackers adopt Medusa ransomware for extortion campaigns, targeting healthcare and nonprofits
entry.content:
Anna Ribeiro reports: A joint investigation by the Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter teams details evidence that operators linked to the Lazarus hacker group are deploying Medusa ransomware in ongoing extortion campaigns targeting the U.S. healthcare sector and a Middle East entity, indicating the North Korean threat cluster continues ransomware-driven extortion campaigns despite prior U.S. indictments.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Greater Pittsburgh Orthopaedic Associates disclosed a 2025 breach, but was there also one in 2024?
entry.content:
Greater Pittsburgh Orthopaedic Associates (GPOA) recently began notifying patients of a breach that occurred on or about August 10, 2025. Although their notification letter to patients does not indicate that this was an incident involving encryption, on August 20, 2025, Ransomhouse had added GPOA to its dark web leak site. Their listing indicated that GPOA... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Attackers Can Own Your Network in a Matter of Minutes
entry.content:
Jai Vijayan reports: In 2025, cybercriminals needed less time to move from break-in to lateral movement across a network than it takes to watch a typical sitcom. An analysis by CrowdStrike of threat activity last year found attackers took just 29 minutes on average to pivot to other systems after gaining an initial foothold in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Ad tech firm Optimizely confirms data breach after vishing attack
entry.content:
New York-based ad tech company Optimizely has notified an undisclosed number of customers of a data breach after threat actors compromised some of its systems in a voice phishing attack. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Spain arrests suspected hacktivists for DDoSing govt sites
entry.content:
Spanish authorities have arrested four alleged members of a hacktivist group believed to have carried out cyberattacks targeting government ministries, political parties, and various public institutions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: North Korean Lazarus group linked to Medusa ransomware attacks
entry.content:
North Korean state-backed hackers associated with the Lazarus threat group are targeting U.S. healthcare organizations in extortion attacks using the Medusa ransomware. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: ShinyHunters extortion gang claims Odido breach affecting millions
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion gang has claimed responsibility for breaching Dutch telecommunications provider Odido and stealing millions of user records from its compromised systems. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CarGurus data breach exposes information of 12.4 million accounts
entry.content:
The ShinyHunters extortion group has published personal information in more than 12 million records allegedly stolen from CarGurus, a U.S.-based digital auto platform. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Wynn Resorts confirms employee data breach after extortion threat
entry.content:
Wynn Resorts has confirmed that a hacker stole employee data from its systems after the company was listed on the ShinyHunters extortion gang's data leak site. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
entry.content:
Granite Wellness Centers in California and Pediatric Home Service in Minnesota have both settled lawsuits stemming from cyberattacks that exposed […] The post Granite Wellness Centers & Pediatric Home Service Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) has temporarily closed most of its clinics following a ransomware attack, and scheduled appointments […] The post UMMC Shuts Clinics While it Grapples with Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media
entry.content:
Healthcare staff need HIPAA training for social media because a single post, photo, or comment can expose Protected Health Information […] The post Why Healthcare Staff Need HIPAA Training for Social Media appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor
entry.content:
Vikor Scientific (now rebranded as Vanta Diagnostics), a molecular diagnostics company based in Charleston, South Carolina, has been affected by […] The post Vikor Scientific Affected by Ransomware Attack on Revenue Cycle Management Vendor appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates Data Breach Affects Almost 57,000 Individuals
entry.content:
Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates has experienced a ransomware attack that has affected almost 57,000 individuals. Data breaches have also been […] The post Greater Pittsburgh Orthopedic Associates Data Breach Affects Almost 57,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
